reference.title: Taffy
reference.shortdesc: Taffy is a chewy confection made from sugar, butter, and flavorings that is produced by repeatedly pulling the candy to incorporate air.

section.title: Overview
section.p: Taffy is characterized by its soft, elastic texture and long shelf life. The pulling process gives taffy its distinctive chewiness and lightened color.

section.title: Common Ingredients
section.p: While recipes vary, taffy typically includes the following ingredients:

section.title: Varieties
section.p: Taffy is produced in many flavors and styles. One well-known variation is saltwater taffy, which is traditionally associated with seaside resorts.
